mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a robust labor market and expanding economy, which translates into higher corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Strong employment typically correlates with economic growth.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS growth is positive but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 40% of observations over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in housing starts signals strong consumer and business confidence, indicating future economic activity and demand for goods and services, which positively impacts corporate revenues and S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month average rises above 800.0 (thousands of units)
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts are a leading economic indicator.
failure_condition: If, when HOUST 3-month average is above 800.0, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative on average or show no statistically significant positive difference from periods below the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising FEDFUNDS and a rising UNRATE signals a deteriorating economic environment, indicating potential stagflation or recessionary pressures that negatively impact corporate earnings and drive S&P 500 forward returns lower over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by 0.25% AND UNRATE rises by 0.5% over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:High interest rates and high unemployment are typically recessionary signals.
failure_condition: If, during periods where both FEDFUNDS and UNRATE have risen significantly over 3 months, the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive on average or not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in retail sales following a period of decline indicates renewed consumer spending and economic recovery, which boosts corporate revenues and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon as market participants price in the recovery.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 1-month growth turns positive after 2 consecutive months of negative growth
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending is a major component of GDP.
failure_condition: If, during a post-shock recovery regime, a positive turn in RSAFS growth does not lead to positive S&P 500 5-day returns in at least 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production signals weakening manufacturing activity and overall economic contraction, leading to lower corporate profits and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a key measure of economic output.
failure_condition: If INDPRO 3-month average growth is negative, but S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market and broader economic weakness, leading to reduced consumer spending and corporate profitability, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by 0.5 percentage points or more over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:Rising unemployment is a strong indicator of recessionary conditions.
failure_condition: If, when UNRATE rises by 0.5 percentage points or more over 3 months, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive on average or not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, erodes purchasing power and corporate profit margins, potentially prompting tighter monetary policy, which negatively impacts equity valuations and drives S&P 500 forward returns lower over a 5-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:High inflation can lead to interest rate hikes and lower equity valuations.
failure_condition: If, when CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth rate exceeds 3.0%, the S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Strong and accelerating GDP growth indicates a robust and expanding economy, which typically translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as investors anticipate future prosperity.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth is above 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:GDP growth is a primary driver of corporate earnings.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth is above 2.5%, but S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 45% of observations over any 5-year rolling window.
